mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is ideal for applications requiring semantic search and matching capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources to handle high loads and the complexity of managing configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Qdrant CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Qdrant instances.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant/releases/download/v1.17.0/qdrant-cli-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/qdrant

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/qdrant

```

> Download the Qdrant CLI binary.
> Make the Qdrant CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Qdrant cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create qdrant-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster for Qdrant.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Qdrant cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials qdrant-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the Qdrant cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Qdrant using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Qdrant on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.tech/helm

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --version 1.17.0 --namespace qdrant --create-namespace

```

> Add the Qdrant Helm repository.
> Install Qdrant using Helm.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Qdrant deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running Qdrant in a managed environment may incur costs based on resource usage.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant deployment and associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

gcloud container clusters delete qdrant-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Qdrant Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of your deployment.

```bash

kubectl cp qdrant/qdrant:/var/lib/qdrant/storage ./backup_storage

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --version 1.17.0 --namespace qdrant

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Backup current storage.
> Upgrade Qdrant to the latest version.
> Verify that the Qdrant pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Qdrant service is not running or the network configuration is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods using 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or reduce the resource requests in the Qdrant deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Storage path not writable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the storage path permissions are not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the storage path has the correct permissions for the Qdrant service account.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Version mismatch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the installed version of Qdrant does not match the expected version in the configuration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Helm chart version matches the Qdrant version specified in your configuration.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.12

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API endpoints for vector search.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API calls to use the new endpoints as per the latest documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and the necessary tools installed.
